name: Mike's Chocolate Caramel Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the cake mix, margarine, and egg until crumbly.
2. Reserve 1½ cups of the mixture and press the remaining into an ungreased 13x9x2 pan for best layering results.
3. Bake at 350°F (175°C) until the crust appears dry (about 10-12 minutes). Let cool for ten minutes.
4. In a saucepan, heat margarine, evaporated milk, and kraft caramels over low heat, stirring constantly until the caramels are melted.
5. Pour the caramel mixture over the crust and sprinkle with chopped nuts (optional).
6. Add the reserved crumbly mixture on top.
7. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 20-30 minutes or until the mixture looks dry again. Let cool for ten minutes.
8. Spread Betty Crockers Deluxe Chocolate icing over the bars. Mix with 3 teaspoons water or enough to make the icing spread easily.
9. Loosen the edges of the bars with a spatula, then frost.
10. Set aside for at least one hour before serving to allow the caramels to stiffen.
Note: This recipe is attributed to Michael Levine (KBSG46A).
